evals.name: multi-list-shared-search
evals.prompt:
Add a shared search input to the FSP members area. The page is at `src/app/admin/benutzer/page.tsx` and renders TWO `InteractiveList` components side-by-side:

- Active members: `src/features/members/fsp/lists/MembersList/`
- Pending invitations: `src/features/members/fsp/lists/MembersPendingInvitationsList/`

Both lists share `src/features/members/fsp/lists/MembersList/MembersListSearchParams.ts`, which currently has only `pagination` with typed keys `members` and `invitedMembers` (so each list paginates independently).

Goal: the user types in one search input → BOTH lists filter by name/email. Each list still paginates independently. The backend supports `q[name_or_email_cont]=<query>` for both endpoints (`/financial_service_provider/memberships` and `/financial_service_provider/invitations/pending`).

Wire it end-to-end:

1. Add `search` to `MembersListSearchParams.ts` (keep the multi-key pagination union typed).
2. Create a standalone `MembersSearchAction` component the first list will render via `renderActions`. Search changes must reset pagination to `null` so both lists go back to page 1.
3. Update both request functions (`src/features/members/backend/getFspMembers.ts` and `src/features/members/backend/getPendingFspInvitations.ts`) so the search param flows through `buildApiUrl`.
4. Update both presentation components for the multi-list pattern (manual pagination via `useQueryState`, only the first list passes `renderActions`).
5. Update `src/app/admin/benutzer/page.tsx` to also destructure `search` from the parsed cache and pass it into both fetches.

Keep the existing page composition shape: the page parses search params, runs both fetches in parallel via `Promise.all`, and renders the Presentations directly. Do NOT introduce per-list server containers or `<Suspense>` wrappers — neither exists on this page today and we don't want them.

The item types (`FspMemberType`, `InvitationType`) and the existing fetch helpers (`getFspMembers`, `getPendingInvitations`) are already in place — don't change those, just thread search through.
evals.expected_output: An end-to-end multi-list shared-search wiring. SearchParams gains `search`. A standalone `MembersSearchAction.tsx` exists. Both request functions pass searchParams to buildApiUrl. Both presentations switch to manual `useQueryState` pagination handling. Only the first list renders `renderActions`. The page extracts `search` and passes it to both fetches.
expectations: MembersListSearchParams.ts declares `search: parseAsString.withDefault("")` alongside the existing pagination
MembersListSearchParams.ts keeps the typed pagination union (Record<{members|invitedMembers}, string>) — does not regress to Record<string, string>
A standalone MembersSearchAction is a React component (function returning JSX), NOT a hook returning a render function
MembersSearchAction uses `useQueryState` for both `search` and `pagination`
MembersSearchAction calls `setPagination(null)` whenever search changes (so both lists return to page 1)
getFspMembers.ts passes `search` through buildApiUrl (either via passing the full searchParams object or including search in the searchParams arg)
getPendingFspInvitations.ts passes `search` through buildApiUrl in the same way
MembersListPresentation.tsx (the first list) passes `renderActions={MembersSearchAction}` to its InteractiveList
MembersPendingInvitationsPresentation.tsx (the second list) does NOT pass `renderActions`
The page (src/app/admin/benutzer/page.tsx) destructures `search` from the parsed search params cache and passes it to both fetch calls
The page does NOT introduce a `<Suspense>` wrapper or per-list server container — keeps the existing inline `Promise.all` composition

evals.name: build-list-with-full-actions
evals.prompt:
Build a new list of submitted financing cases for the operations portal, end to end:

- Feature path: `src/features/financingCases/lists/SubmittedFinancingCasesList/`
- Request: `src/features/financingCases/fsp/backend/getSubmittedFinancingCases.ts`
- Page: `src/app/operations/eingereicht/page.tsx`

Endpoint: `GET /financial_service_providers/financing_cases/submitted`. Use `getPaginatedFspFinancingCases` from `@/features/financingCases/fsp/backend/server`. Item type: `FspFinancingCase` from `@/features/financingCases/fsp/backend/schema`.

The list needs the following actions:

- **Search** — query param `search_term`.
- **Sort** — values `created_at asc`, `created_at desc`, `submitted_at asc`, `submitted_at desc`.
- **Group by status** — query param `q[status_eq]`, values `pending | under_review | accepted | rejected`.
- **Reset** — clear all of the above.

The presentation can render minimal rows (just the case ID and status — no styling effort needed). Focus on getting the SearchParams, GroupConfigs, request file, container, presentation, items hooks, and the `useSubmittedFinancingCasesRenderActions` composition right. The page should parse searchParams once and render the server container directly — do not introduce a `<Suspense>` wrapper (that's not the pattern this codebase uses for single-list pages).
evals.expected_output: A complete new list with all four actions wired up. SearchParams declares pagination + search + sortBy enum + groupBy enum + status sub-enum. A GroupConfigs file maps each groupBy enum value to its query param + sub-enum values. The request file uses the if-else shape switching between `collectGroupedData` and `collectPaginatedData`. The container is a server component. The presentation uses `usePagination` and renders `<InteractiveList renderActions={...} />`. The renderActions hook composes SearchAction + SortByAction + GroupByAction + ResetAction inside a VStack/ActionRow layout.
expectations: A SearchParams file is created and declares pagination (createBase64Parser), search (parseAsString.withDefault("")), sortBy (parseAsStringEnum), groupBy (parseAsStringEnum)
SortByEnum has values for `created_at asc`, `created_at desc`, `submitted_at asc`, `submitted_at desc`
GroupByEnum has a STATUS value (and any other group dimensions if applicable)
A status sub-enum (e.g., SubmittedFinancingCasesGroupByStatusEnum) is declared with values pending, under_review, accepted, rejected
createSearchParamsCache and the parsed type are exported from the SearchParams file

A GroupConfigs file is created and maps GroupByEnum.STATUS to `{ queryParam: 'q[status_eq]', values: Object.values(<StatusEnum>) }` (the GroupConfig type only has queryParam + values; translated headings are built inline in the request file as a `titles: Record<string, string>` map)
The request file (getSubmittedFinancingCases.ts) uses the if-else pattern: if groupBy is set, call collectGroupedData; else call collectPaginatedData
The request file pre-initializes `listItems` before the if-else block
The request file passes the destructured fields (search, sortBy, etc.) into buildApiUrl's searchParams arg
The container is an async server component with no `'use client'` directive
The container imports the request and passes its result as `data` to the Presentation
use{ListName}SortByItems starts with an empty-string "no sort" entry
use{ListName}GroupByItems starts with a "no grouping" entry — either empty string or `GroupByEnum.NONE` value
useSubmittedFinancingCasesRenderActions calls `useListActions` with `searchParams`, `translations.label`, and `children`
The composition renders <SearchAction />, <SortByAction />, <GroupByAction />, <ResetAction />
The composition uses VStack and/or ActionRow for layout
The composition does NOT render <ArchivedAction /> (not requested for this list)
The presentation uses usePagination({ parserBuilder, listItems }) and renders <InteractiveList />
The presentation passes `renderActions={renderActions}` from useSubmittedFinancingCasesRenderActions
The page imports and renders the container (no Suspense wrapper)

evals.name: build-date-range-action
evals.prompt:
Build a new shared `DateRangeAction` slot for `useListActions`. It should be reusable across any list — the same way `SearchAction`, `GroupByAction`, etc. are.

**File**: `src/shared/components/RenderActions/DateRangeAction.tsx`

**URL state shape**: `dateRange = { from: string; to: string } | null` where `from` and `to` are ISO date strings (`YYYY-MM-DD`).

**Backend mapping**: `from` → `q[created_at_gteq]=<from>`, `to` → `q[created_at_lteq]=<to>`. When the field is `null` or unset, no params should be emitted. Implement this generically inside `src/shared/backend/models/common/buildApiUrl.ts` (so any list that declares `dateRange` on its searchParams gets the mapping for free, mirroring how `archived` is handled today).

**SearchParams type**: extend `BaseSearchParams` in `src/shared/types/searchParams.ts` to declare `dateRange` as an OPTIONAL field, so lists that don't need it aren't forced to declare it. Use `parseAsJson<{ from: string; to: string } | null>()` for the parser builder type.

**Slot contract**: follow the same pattern as `ArchivedAction` and `SearchAction` — reads `dateRange` parser from `useListActionsContext`, throws if it's missing on the list's searchParams, registers itself for global Reset, persists across navigation via `usePersistedFilters`, resets pagination on every change. The component is `"use client"`.

For the visible UI, two `<Input type="date" />` controls inside a `<Box flexBasis="100%">` is enough — the focus is on the contract and the API mapping, not date-picker UX.
evals.expected_output: Three artifacts: (1) a new `DateRangeAction.tsx` that follows the six-step slot contract; (2) `BaseSearchParams` updated to include `dateRange?: ParserBuilder<{ from: string; to: string } | null>`; (3) `buildApiUrl.ts` extended with a generic branch that emits `q[created_at_gteq]` / `q[created_at_lteq]` when the `dateRange` field is present and non-null on the searchParams object.
expectations: DateRangeAction.tsx has the `"use client"` directive
DateRangeAction uses useListActionsContext() to get startTransition, setPagination, searchParams, registerFilter
DateRangeAction throws an Error when searchParams.dateRange is undefined
DateRangeAction calls useQueryState("dateRange", searchParams.dateRange.withOptions({ shallow: false, startTransition, throttleMs: 500 }))
DateRangeAction registers itself via useEffect → registerFilter({ key: "dateRange", reset: () => setDateRange(null) })
DateRangeAction calls usePersistedFilters with dateRange in setters/currentValues/defaultValues
DateRangeAction's change handler calls setDateRange(...) AND setPagination(null), wrapped in startTransition
DateRangeAction renders two <Input type="date" /> controls (one for from, one for to)
BaseSearchParams (src/shared/types/searchParams.ts) adds an OPTIONAL field `dateRange?: ParserBuilder<{ from: string; to: string } | null>`
buildApiUrl.ts adds a typed interface (similar to SearchParamsWithArchived) for dateRange
buildApiUrl.ts emits `q[created_at_gteq]=<from>` and `q[created_at_lteq]=<to>` when dateRange is present and non-null on searchParams
buildApiUrl.ts does NOT emit those query params when dateRange is null or missing
The mapping logic lives in buildApiUrl, NOT inside DateRangeAction.tsx (keeps slot feature-agnostic)

evals.name: decouple-archived-action
evals.prompt:
The shared `ArchivedAction` at `src/shared/components/RenderActions/ArchivedAction.tsx` currently imports `FSPFinancingCasesGroupByEnum` and hardcodes the side effect of forcing `groupBy = NONE` when archived is on (and restoring `groupBy = STATUS` when archived is off). This couples a shared component to a feature-specific enum.

Refactor:

1. In `src/shared/components/RenderActions/ArchivedAction.tsx`:
   - Remove the `FSPFinancingCasesGroupByEnum` import.
   - Remove the `setGroupBy` block entirely. The shared component should not touch `groupBy`.
   - Add an optional callback prop: `onArchivedChange?: (archived: boolean) => void`. Call it inside the `startTransition` block, after `setArchived` and `setPagination(null)`.
   - Keep everything else identical: the throw-when-`archived`-is-missing check, the `useQueryState` binding, the `registerFilter` call, the `<ToggleGroup />` UI.

2. In `src/features/financingCases/lists/FSPFinancingCasesList/useFSPFinancingCasesRenderActions.tsx`:
   - Wire the `onArchivedChange` callback so the FSP-specific behavior moves out of the shared component: when archived becomes `true`, set `groupBy` to `FSPFinancingCasesGroupByEnum.NONE`; when it becomes `false`, set `groupBy` to `FSPFinancingCasesGroupByEnum.STATUS`. Use a `useQueryState` for `groupBy` at the consumer level so you have the setter to call from the callback.
   - Don't change the rest of the composition (the existing search/group/sort/archived/reset slots, the `processedGroupByItems` disable logic for STATUS, the layout) — only thread the callback through.
evals.expected_output: Two updated files. `ArchivedAction.tsx` no longer imports any feature-specific enum and has an `onArchivedChange?: (archived: boolean) => void` prop. The cross-action effect lives entirely in `useFSPFinancingCasesRenderActions.tsx`, which acquires a `setGroupBy` setter and passes the callback to `<ArchivedAction />`.
expectations: ArchivedAction.tsx no longer imports FSPFinancingCasesGroupByEnum (or any other feature-specific enum)
ArchivedAction.tsx no longer has a useQueryState for `groupBy` and no longer calls setGroupBy
ArchivedAction.tsx adds an optional `onArchivedChange?: (archived: boolean) => void` prop
ArchivedAction.tsx's handleToggle (or change handler) calls onArchivedChange?.(...) after setArchived and setPagination(null), inside the startTransition block
ArchivedAction.tsx keeps the throw-when-archived-is-missing check intact
ArchivedAction.tsx keeps the registerFilter useEffect intact
ArchivedAction.tsx keeps the <ToggleGroup /> UI unchanged
useFSPFinancingCasesRenderActions.tsx adds a useQueryState for groupBy to acquire setGroupBy

useFSPFinancingCasesRenderActions.tsx passes onArchivedChange to <ArchivedAction /> that sets groupBy to FSPFinancingCasesGroupByEnum.NONE when archived is true and FSPFinancingCasesGroupByEnum.STATUS when archived is false
useFSPFinancingCasesRenderActions.tsx still composes <SearchAction />, <GroupByAction />, <SortByAction />, <ArchivedAction />, <ResetAction />
useFSPFinancingCasesRenderActions.tsx preserves the processedGroupByItems STATUS-disable logic (when archived) — that pre-processing stays at the consumer level
useFSPFinancingCasesRenderActions.tsx preserves the existing VStack + ActionRow layout
